title: hieracium pellitum subsp. oligocephalum
intro: Hieracium pellitum subsp. oligocephalum, also known as Hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion family, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Hieracium pellitum subsp. oligocephalum include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to support overall well-being and alleviate various health complaints.
